devanagari: ब्रह्मोवाच । इति तद्गृणतां तेषां मुनीनां योगधर्मिणाम् । प्रतिनन्द्य जगादेदं विकुण्ठनिलयो विभुः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmovāca iti tadgṛṇatāṃ teṣāṃ munīnāṃ yogadharmiṇām pratinandya jagādedaṃ vikuṇṭhanilayo vibhuḥ

translation:
Brahmā said:
While the sages who were by nature Bhakti-yogis were singing his eulogy, the All-powerful Lord whose abode was in Vaikuṇṭha, received them cheerfully and spoke as follows:

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । एतौ तौ पार्षदौ मह्यं जयो विजय एव च । कदर्थीकृत्य मां यद्वो बह्वक्रातामतिक्रमम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca etau tau pārṣadau mahyaṃ jayo vijaya eva ca kadarthīkṛtya māṃ yadvo bahvakrātāmatikramam

translation:
The Lord said:
Oh sages, as these two servants of mine, Jaya and Vijaya, disregarding me, have gravely insulted you and slighted gods, I have approved of the punishment meted out to them by you who perform penance for me.

devanagari: यस्त्वेतयोर्धृतो दण्डो भवद्भिर्मामनुव्रतैः । स एवानुमतोऽस्माभिः मुनयो देवहेलनात् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: yastvetayordhṛto daṇḍo bhavadbhirmāmanuvrataiḥ sa evānumato

translation:
The Lord said:
Oh sages, as these two servants of mine, Jaya and Vijaya, disregarding me, have gravely insulted you and slighted gods, I have approved of the punishment meted out to them by you who perform penance for me.

devanagari: तद्वः प्रसादयाम्यद्य ब्रह्म दैवं परं हि मे । तद्धीत्यात्मकृतं मन्ये यत्स्वपुम्भिरसत्कृताः ॥ ४ ॥

translation: I, therefore, apologize to you today, for Brāhmaṇa is my highest deity. I regard that the offence given to you by my servants is as good as done by me.

devanagari: यन्नामानि च गृह्णाति लोको भृत्ये कृतागसि । सोऽसाधुवादस्तत् कीर्तिं हन्ति त्वचमिवामयः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: yannāmāni ca gṛhṇāti loko bhṛtye kṛtāgasi so
translation: When a servant has committed an offence, the world takes the name of the master. That blame spoils the reputation of the master as white leprosy ḍispigments the skin.

devanagari: यस्यामृतामलयशःश्रवणावगाहः । सद्यः पुनाति जगदाश्वपचाद्विकुण्ठः । सोऽहं भवद्‍भ्य उपलब्धसुतीर्थकीर्तिः । छिन्द्यां स्वबाहुमपि वः प्रतिकूलवृ...
english_devnagari: yasyāmṛtāmalayaśaḥśravaṇāvagāhaḥ sadyaḥ punāti jagadāśvapacādvikuṇṭhaḥ so chindyāṃ svabāhumapi vaḥ pratikūlavṛttim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural]

translation: I am that Vaikuṇṭha by hearing whose nectarlike, pure glory, the world down to Cāṇḍāla is instantly sanctified. I who have obtained such excellent purifying reputation from honourable persons like you, would sever even my own arms if they be of a hostile nature to you.

devanagari: यत्सेवया चरणपद्मपवित्ररेणुं । सद्यः क्षताखिलमलं प्रतिलब्धशीलम् । न श्रीर्विरक्तमपि मां विजहाति यस्याः । प्रेक्षालवार्थ इतरे नियमान् वहन्...
english_devnagari: yatsevayā caraṇapadmapavitrareṇuṃ sadyaḥ kṣatākhilamalaṃ pratilabdhaśīlam na śrīrviraktamapi māṃ vijahāti yasyāḥ prekṣālavārtha itare niyamān vahanti

[nominative plural from √ vah class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vah class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vah class 1 verb]

translation: By serving whom (Brāhmaṇas) I have got even the dust on my lotus-like feet so sanctifying as to purge the whole world of all impurities and have got such a good nature established in me that the goddess Lakṣmī, for the favour of whose passing glance others (i.e. gods like Brahmā etc.) observe rules (of penance), does not leave me even though I am not attached to her.

devanagari: नाहं तथाद्मि यजमानहविर्वितानेः । च्योतद्‍घृतप्लुतमदन् हुतभुङ्मुखेन । यद्‍ब्राह्मणस्य मुखतश्चरतोऽनुघासं । तुष्टस्य मय्यवहितैर्निजकर्मपाकै...
english_devnagari: nāhaṃ tathādmi yajamānahavirvitāneḥ cyotad‍ghṛtaplutamadan hutabhuṅmukhena yad‍brāhmaṇasya mukhataścarato tuṣṭasya mayyavahitairnijakarmapākaiḥ
translation: I do not enjoy with that much relish the sacrificial oblations offered by a performer of sacrifice as I do the ghee- dripping food through the mouth of a Brāhmaṇa who has offered all the fruits of his actions tome and who eats every morsel of that food with satisfaction.

devanagari: येषां बिभर्म्यहमखण्डविकुण्ठयोग । मायाविभूतिरमलाङ्घ्रिरजः किरीटैः । विप्रांस्तु को न विषहेत यदर्हणाम्भः । सद्यः पुनाति सहचन्द्रललामलोकान्...
english_devnagari: yeṣāṃ bibharmyahamakhaṇḍavikuṇṭhayoga māyāvibhūtiramalāṅghrirajaḥ kirīṭaiḥ viprāṃstu ko na viṣaheta yadarhaṇāmbhaḥ sadyaḥ punāti sahacandralalāmalokān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: [1] Who will not tolerate the Brāhmaṇas the dust of whose holy feet I bear on my crowns (in all my incarnations) though I am endowed with the power of the infinite and unrestrained yoga-māyā and though the water used for washing my feet in worship instantly sanctifies the world including god Siva (who bears the Moon as an ornament on the forehead).
purport:
Footnote [1]:
VC. construes differently: ‘It is due to my bearing the dust of the holy feet of Brāhmaṇas that I became possessed of the excellence of the Yoga-māyā, etc.

devanagari: ये मे तनूर्द्विजवरान्दुहतीर्मदीया । भूतान्यलब्धशरणानि च भेदबुद्ध्या । द्रक्ष्यन्त्यघक्षतदृशो ह्यहिमन्यवस्तान् । गृध्रा रुषा मम कुषन्त्य...
english_devnagari: ye me tanūrdvijavarānduhatīrmadīyā bhūtānyalabdhaśaraṇāni ca bhedabuddhyā drakṣyantyaghakṣatadṛśo hyahimanyavastān gṛdhrā ruṣā mama kuṣantyadhidaṇḍanetuḥ

translation: Brāhmaṇas, milk-yielding cows and protection-less beings are my own person (body). Those who look upon them as different from me, get themselves torn with rage by the bills of vulture-like servants of Yama who is appointed for dispensation of punishment—servants whose anger is like serpents.

devanagari: ये ब्राह्मणान्मयि धिया क्षिपतोऽर्चयन्तः । तुष्यद्धृदः स्मितसुधोक्षितपद्मवक्त्राः । वाण्यानुरागकलयात्मजवद् गृणन्तः । सम्बोधयन्ति अहमिवाह...
english_devnagari: ye brāhmaṇānmayi dhiyā kṣipato tuṣyaddhṛdaḥ smitasudhokṣitapadmavaktrāḥ vāṇyānurāgakalayātmajavad gṛṇantaḥ sambodhayanti ahamivāhamupāhṛtastaiḥ

translation: I am won over by them who, looking upon even harsh-speaking Brāhmaṇas as me (Hari) address and honour them with a joyous heart, and with their lotus-like faces beaming over (lit. sprinkled) with nectar-like (sweet) smiles, praise them in affectionate terms like a (loving) son unto (his angry) father (or an affectionate father to his excited son) or as I have done (to Bhṛgu or to you).

devanagari: तन्मे स्वभर्तुरवसायमलक्षमाणौ । युष्मद्व्यतिक्रमगतिं प्रतिपद्य सद्यः । भूयो ममान्तिकमितां तदनुग्रहो मे । यत्कल्पतामचिरतो भृतयोर्विवासः ॥...
english_devnagari: tanme svabharturavasāyamalakṣamāṇau yuṣmadvyatikramagatiṃ pratipadya sadyaḥ bhūyo mamāntikamitāṃ tadanugraho me yatkalpatāmacirato bhṛtayorvivāsaḥ

[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: It will be a favour to me if these two servants who did not understand the intention of their Lord (me) should again return to my presence after immediately undergoing the lower stage for insulting you. May the banishement of the servants terminate before long.[2]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Tat... vivāsaḥ [vivāsa]: (i) May their special residence with me (as master and servant) be restored soon—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā, VC.
(ii) Bhāgavata Candrikā: It is my grace due to which these servants, undergoing the punishment for their misdemeanour will return to me within a short period.
(iii) Had it not been my grace, they would never escape the Asura-yoni due to Braḥmanicaī curse. Their early return after completion of their term in Asura-life is due to my favour. The word hi denotes the consent of Sanaka and other sages for this way out of the curse.

devanagari: ब्रह्मोवाच । अथ तस्योशतीं देवीं ऋषिकुल्यां सरस्वतीम् । नास्वाद्य मन्युदष्टानां तेषां आत्माप्यतृप्यत ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmovāca atha tasyośatīṃ devīṃ ṛṣikulyāṃ sarasvatīm nāsvādya manyudaṣṭānāṃ teṣāṃ ātmāpyatṛpyata

translation:
Brahmā said:
At that time, the mind of those sages which was over-whelmed with (the poisonous snake bite in the from of) rage was not satisfied even though they tasted (i.e. heard the sweetness of) the Lord’s attractive and brilliant speech composed of a stream of Mantras—speech similar to river Sarasvatī which is lovely, heavenly, suitable to the assembly of sages.

devanagari: सतीं व्यादाय शृण्वन्तो लघ्वीं गुर्वर्थगह्वराम् । विगाह्यागाधगम्भीरां न विदुस्तच्चिकीर्षितम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: satīṃ vyādāya śṛṇvanto laghvīṃ gurvarthagahvarām vigāhyāgādhagambhīrāṃ na vidustaccikīrṣitam

[nominative single from √ kṛ ], [accusative single from √ kṛ ]

translation: Giving ear with close attention to the true but beautiful speech which was brief yet difficult to comprehend due to its weighty import, unfathomable intention and deep significance, they did not understand what he wished to do, despite their consideration.

devanagari: ते योगमाययारब्ध पारमेष्ठ्यमहोदयम् । प्रोचुः प्राञ्जलयो विप्राः प्रहृष्टाः क्षुभितत्वचः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: te yogamāyayārabdha pārameṣṭhyamahodayam procuḥ prāñjalayo viprāḥ prahṛṣṭāḥ kṣubhitatvacaḥ

translation: The highly delighted Brāhmaṇas with hair standing on their ends (on their skin) folded their hands and addressed to him who had manifested the highest glory of his supreme authority by his yoga-māyā.

devanagari: ऋषय ऊचुः । न वयं भगवन् विद्मः तव देव चिकीर्षितम् । कृतो मेऽनुग्रहश्चेति यदध्यक्षः प्रभाषसे ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛṣaya ūcuḥ na vayaṃ bhagavan vidmaḥ tava deva cikīrṣitam kṛto me

translation:
The sages said:
Oh god, we do not understand your implication when you, the Supreme Lord say (to us),‘You have done me a fovour! (lit. favour has been done to me by you)’.

devanagari: ब्रह्मण्यस्य परं दैवं ब्राह्मणाः किल ते प्रभो । विप्राणां देवदेवानां भगवान् आत्मदैवतम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmaṇyasya paraṃ daivaṃ brāhmaṇāḥ kila te prabho viprāṇāṃ devadevānāṃ bhagavān ātmadaivatam
translation: It is reported that to you, the protector of Brāhmaṇas, Brāhmaṇas are the supreme deity. But you, the supreme Lord, are both the soul and the God unto the Brāhmaṇas who are adorable to gods.

devanagari: त्वत्तः सनातनो धर्मो रक्ष्यते तनुभिस्तव । धर्मस्य परमो गुह्यो निर्विकारो भवान्मतः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: tvattaḥ sanātano dharmo rakṣyate tanubhistava dharmasya paramo guhyo nirvikāro bhavānmataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: You are the source of eternal Dharma. It is protected ed by your forms. You are regarded as the highest fruit (of Dharma and hence) deserve to be kept secret (—a fruit not perishable like the attainment of heaven) and you are changeless and imperishable.

devanagari: तरन्ति ह्यञ्जसा मृत्युं निवृत्ता यदनुग्रहात् । योगिनः स भवान् किंस्विद् अनुगृह्येत यत्परैः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: taranti hyañjasā mṛtyuṃ nivṛttā yadanugrahāt yoginaḥ sa bhavān kiṃsvid anugṛhyeta yatparaiḥ

translation: How is it conceivable that Your honour is to be favoured by others—Your Honour by whose grace the Yogins become unattached (to the world) and easily transgress (the saṃsāra—cycle of birth and) death.

devanagari: यं वै विभूतिरुपयात्यनुवेलमन्यैः । अर्थार्थिभिः स्वशिरसा धृतपादरेणुः । धन्यार्पिताङ्घ्रितुलसीनवदामधाम्नो । लोकं मधुव्रतपतेरिव कामयाना ॥ ...

translation: [3] The Goddess Lakṣmī, the dust on whose feet is applied to their heads by others desirous of attaining different Puruṣārthas, appears to crave for the place of the black-bee chief whose dwelling is in fresh Tulasī garland offered (in worship) to your feet by the blessed ones. Hence verily she serves you every now and then.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
This refers to supra III, 16.7.

devanagari: यस्तां विविक्तचरितैः अनुवर्तमानां । नात्याद्रियत्परमभागवतप्रसङ्गः । स त्वं द्विजानुपथपुण्यरजः पुनीतः । श्रीवत्सलक्ष्म किमगा भगभाजनस्त्व...
english_devnagari: yastāṃ viviktacaritaiḥ anuvartamānāṃ nātyādriyatparamabhāgavataprasaṅgaḥ sa tvaṃ dvijānupathapuṇyarajaḥ punītaḥ śrīvatsalakṣma kimagā bhagabhājanastvam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[imperfect active second single], [aorist active second single]

translation: You who are highly attached to your great devotees, do not show much regard to goddess Lakṣmī who waits upon you by her pious acts of worship (which nobody else can perform. You are the receptacle of all excellences[4]. Such as you are, are you purified by the dust on the road sanctified by the feet of the Brāhmaṇas and by the mark of Śrī Vatsa? Do these add to your adornments?
purport:
Footnote [4]:
You are absolutely pure as you are the receptacle of all attributes worth possessing. Do the dust on streets traversed by Brāhmaṇas and (he Śrī Vatsa mark (left by the kick of Bhṛgu) sanctify you? Or do you regard them as ornaments?—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: धर्मस्य ते भगवतस्त्रियुग त्रिभिः स्वैः । पद्भिश्चराचरमिदं द्विजदेवतार्थम् । नूनं भृतं तदभिघाति रजस्तमश्च । सत्त्वेन नो वरदया तनुवा निरस...
english_devnagari: dharmasya te bhagavatastriyuga tribhiḥ svaiḥ padbhiścarācaramidaṃ dvijadevatārtham nūnaṃ bhṛtaṃ tadabhighāti rajastamaśca sattvena no varadayā tanuvā nirasya
translation: Oh Triyuga![5] It is really for the sake of Brāhmaṇas and gods that you, the Supreme Lord, Dharma incarnate, have protected this world of movables and immovables by your three feet (viz. Tapas—penance, Śauca—purity, and Dayā—mercy). By means of Sat tva, your body which favours us with blessings,[6] you have done away with the attributes rajas and tamas which obstruct Dharma.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
tri-yuga—(i) He who manifests himself as avatāra in three-yuga period.
(ii) One who possesses six excellences viz., knowledge, power etc.—the characteristics of bhagavān—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā, Bhāgavata Candrikā, VC., etc.

Footnote [6]:
varadayā—By your body (avatāra) called Kapila—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: न त्वं द्विजोत्तमकुलं यदिहात्मगोपं । गोप्ता वृषः स्वर्हणेन ससूनृतेन । तर्ह्येव नङ्क्ष्यति शिवस्तव देव पन्था । लोकोऽग्रहीष्यद् ऋषभस्य हि...
english_devnagari: na tvaṃ dvijottamakulaṃ yadihātmagopaṃ goptā vṛṣaḥ svarhaṇena sasūnṛtena tarhyeva naṅkṣyati śivastava deva panthā loko

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ hā class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ hā class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ hā class 1 verb]

translation: Oh God, if you do not protect by sweet words and proper honour, the race of the best of Brāhmaṇas who deserve specially your protection, then your own auspicious path (of Vedas) will be lost. For people will regard that behaviour of the most excellent (person like you) as the standard (to follow).

devanagari: तत्तेऽनभीष्टमिव सत्त्वनिधेर्विधित्सोः । क्षेमं जनाय निजशक्तिभिरुद्धृतारेः । नैतावता त्र्यधिपतेर्बत विश्वभर्तुः । तेजः क्षतं त्ववनतस्य स...
english_devnagari: tatte kṣemaṃ janāya nijaśaktibhiruddhṛtāreḥ naitāvatā tryadhipaterbata viśvabhartuḥ tejaḥ kṣataṃ tvavanatasya sa te vinodaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṣan class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ kṣan class 8 verb]

translation: That (loss of Vedic path) is not at all desired by you. You are an ocean of the attribute of Sattva. You who have destroyed your adversaries by your powers, desire to achieve the good of the world. You are the ruler of the three worlds and protector of the universe. By such bowing (to the Brāhmaṇas) your splendour docs not diminish. It is for the guidance of the world.

devanagari: यं वानयोर्दममधीश भवान्विधत्ते । वृत्तिं नु वा तदनुमन्महि निर्व्यलीकम् । अस्मासु वा य उचितो ध्रियतां स दण्डो । येऽनागसौ वयमयुङ्क्ष्महि क...
english_devnagari: yaṃ vānayordamamadhīśa bhavānvidhatte vṛttiṃ nu vā tadanumanmahi nirvyalīkam asmāsu vā ya ucito dhriyatāṃ sa daṇḍo ye

translation: Oh Supreme Lord, we shall sincerely approve of whatever punishment or respectful treatment your honour will mete out to both of these. Or you may inflict suitable punishment on us who have pronounced the curse on those innocent door-keepers.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । एतौ सुरेतरगतिं प्रतिपद्य सद्यः । संरम्भसम्भृतसमाध्यनुबद्धयोगौ । भूयः सकाशमुपयास्यत आशु यो वः । शापो मयैव निमितस्तदवैत व...
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca etau suretaragatiṃ pratipadya sadyaḥ saṃrambhasambhṛtasamādhyanubaddhayogau bhūyaḥ sakāśamupayāsyata āśu yo vaḥ śāpo mayaiva nimitastadavaita viprāḥ

translation:
The Lord said:
“Oh Brāhmaṇas, please note that this curse of yours was ordained by me. These two will immediately take birth as non-heavenly beings (Daityas). Having developed their yoga by concentration intensified by wrath, both these will soon return to me.”

devanagari: ब्रह्मोवाच । अथ ते मुनयो दृष्ट्वा नयनानन्दभाजनम् । वैकुण्ठं तदधिष्ठानं विकुण्ठं च स्वयंप्रभम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmovāca atha te munayo dṛṣṭvā nayanānandabhājanam vaikuṇṭhaṃ tadadhiṣṭhānaṃ vikuṇṭhaṃ ca svayaṃprabham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Brahmā said:
“Then the sages, having seen Viṣṇu, the receptacle of delight to the eyes, and his abode, the self-illuminating Vaikuṇṭha, circumambulated the Lord, bowed down to him. Taking leave of him, they returned, full of joy, praising the glory of Viṣṇu.

devanagari: भगवन्तं परिक्रम्य प्रणिपत्यानुमान्य च । प्रतिजग्मुः प्रमुदिताः शंसन्तो वैष्णवीं श्रियम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavantaṃ parikramya praṇipatyānumānya ca pratijagmuḥ pramuditāḥ śaṃsanto vaiṣṇavīṃ śriyam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ śaṃs class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ śaṃs class 1 verb]

translation:
Brahmā said:
“Then the sages, having seen Viṣṇu, the receptacle of delight to the eyes, and his abode, the self-illuminating Vaikuṇṭha, circumambulated the Lord, bowed down to him. Taking leave of him, they returned, full of joy, praising the glory of Viṣṇu.

devanagari: भगवाननुगावाह यातं मा भैष्टमस्तु शम् । ब्रह्मतेजः समर्थोऽपि हन्तुं नेच्छे मतं तु मे ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavānanugāvāha yātaṃ mā bhaiṣṭamastu śam brahmatejaḥ samartho

translation: Lord Viṣṇu said to his servants, “You go (now). Do not be afraid. May you be happy. Though I am competent to do so, I do not wish to counteract the power of Brāhmaṇas, as it is my will.

devanagari: एतत्पुरैव निर्दिष्टं रमया क्रुद्धया यदा । पुरापवारिता द्वारि विशन्ती मय्युपारते ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: etatpuraiva nirdiṣṭaṃ ramayā kruddhayā yadā purāpavāritā dvāri viśantī mayyupārate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative dual from √ viś class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ viś class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ viś class 6 verb]

translation: Formerly this has been ordained by enraged Laksmī whom you prevented at the door from entering as I was then absorbed in Yogic sleep.

devanagari: मयि संरम्भयोगेन निस्तीर्य ब्रह्महेलनम् । प्रत्येष्यतं निकाशं मे कालेनाल्पीयसा पुनः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: mayi saṃrambhayogena nistīrya brahmahelanam pratyeṣyataṃ nikāśaṃ me kālenālpīyasā punaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ il class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ il class 6 verb]

translation: Within a short period of time, you will return to my presence after undergoing the curse of Brahmanical insult by means of your concentration in me due to anger.”

devanagari: द्वाःस्थावादिश्य भगवान् विमानश्रेणिभूषणम् । सर्वातिशयया लक्ष्म्या जुष्टं स्वं धिष्ण्यमाविशत् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: dvāḥsthāvādiśya bhagavān vimānaśreṇibhūṣaṇam sarvātiśayayā lakṣmyā juṣṭaṃ svaṃ dhiṣṇyamāviśat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]

[nominative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]

translation: Having ordered the door-keepers thus, Lord Viṣṇu entered into his abode which appeared beautiful by a row of heavenly cars and which was endowed with splendour excelling all[7].
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Which surpassed everything else in beauty as it was meant for goddess Lakṣmī—Subodhinī
Or, He entered his mansion along with Lakṣmī who excelled all—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: तौ तु गीर्वाणऋषभौ दुस्तरात् हरिलोकतः । हतश्रियौ ब्रह्मशापाद् अभूतां विगतस्मयौ ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tau tu gīrvāṇaṛṣabhau dustarāt harilokataḥ hataśriyau brahmaśāpād abhūtāṃ vigatasmayau
translation: Those two prominent gods (Jaya and Vijaya) whose glory and pride deserted them due to the irrevocable Brahmanical curse, fell down from Vaikuṇṭha (Hari’s abode).

devanagari: तदा विकुण्ठधिषणात् तयोर्निपतमानयोः । हाहाकारो महानासीद् विमानाग्र्येषु पुत्रकाः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: tadā vikuṇṭhadhiṣaṇāt tayornipatamānayoḥ hāhākāro mahānāsīd vimānāgryeṣu putrakāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation: Oh children (Gods), while they were thus falling down from the region of Vaikuṇṭha, there was a loud cry expressing grief (Alas! Alas!) from great persons in heavenly cars.

devanagari: तावेव ह्यधुना प्राप्तौ पार्षदप्रवरौ हरेः । दितेर्जठरनिर्विष्टं काश्यपं तेज उल्बणम् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: tāveva hyadhunā prāptau pārṣadapravarau hareḥ diterjaṭharanirviṣṭaṃ kāśyapaṃ teja ulbaṇam
translation: And those very prominent attendants of Hari have now entered the powerful lustre (semen) of Kāśyapa deposited in the womb of Diti.

devanagari: तयोरसुरयोरद्य तेजसा यमयोर्हि वः । आक्षिप्तं तेज एतर्हि भगवान् तद्विधित्सति ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: tayorasurayoradya tejasā yamayorhi vaḥ ākṣiptaṃ teja etarhi bhagavān tadvidhitsati
translation: It is by the lustre of the twin-Asuras that your glory has been eclipsed. And Lord Viṣṇu himself wills it.

devanagari: विश्वस्य यः स्थितिलयोद्‍भवहेतुराद्यो । योगेश्वरैरपि दुरत्यययोगमायः । क्षेमं विधास्यति स नो भगवांस्त्र्यधीशः । तत्रास्मदीयविमृशेन कियानि...
english_devnagari: viśvasya yaḥ sthitilayod‍bhavaheturādyo yogeśvarairapi duratyayayogamāyaḥ kṣemaṃ vidhāsyati sa no bhagavāṃstryadhīśaḥ tatrāsmadīyavimṛśena kiyānihārthaḥ
translation:
The Lord (Viṣṇu), the supreme ruler of the three worlds, the First Being, the cause of the creation, maintenance and destruction of the universe, whose Yoga-māyā cannot be transgressed by masters of Yoga, will do what is good to us. Of what use (effect) is our thought (brooding) in that matter?
